articles.title: Argentina downs Austria, Messi sets World Cup scoring record
articles.description: ARLINGTON, United States — Lionel Messi became the leading scorer in World Cup history as the captain struck twice to give Argentina a 2-0 win over Austria on Monday and send the champions into the last 32. The player widely regarded as the greatest of all time pounced late in the first half in Texas with a trademark left-footed finish after neat build-up play. The predatory goal added to his hat-trick in Argentina’s opening match to make it a record 17 in total at the World Cup. READ: Magical Messi equals World Cup goals record as Argentina wins The 38-year-old [...]... Keep on reading: Argentina downs Austria, Messi sets World Cup scoring record

articles.title: Iran national anthem booed by sections of crowd before World Cup match
articles.description: Sections of the crowd at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, California, were heard booing during the Iranian national anthem ahead of Iran's World Cup match against Belgium on June 21, 2026. Video from the stadium captures the reaction during the pre-match ceremony before the Group G fixture. The match was Iran's second game of the 2026 FIFA World Cup and was played in front of a large crowd at the Los Angeles-area venue.

articles.description: Venus and Serena Williams recently took the tennis world by storm by accepting a doubles wildcard for the 2026 Wimbledon Championships, reviving their iconic partnership that includes 14 Grand Slam doubles titles and three Olympic gold medals.
